title: Within-lap ranking with soft relevance based on remaining laps to stop
summary: Public ranking docs show CatBoost supports querywise and pairwise objectives inside groups.
rationale: Public ranking docs show CatBoost supports querywise and pairwise objectives inside groups. The current rank-style attempts appear to use hard next-lap labels; that throws away ordering information among cars that will stop in 2, 3, or 4 laps. Treating each race-lap as a choice set with graded relevance is closer to the real strategist decision.
implementation_hint: Group by Year-Race-LapNumber. Define soft relevance from future stop distance, e.g. 1.0 for next lap, then decaying values for 2-4 laps, or direct inverse remaining-laps. Train CatBoost with QuerySoftMax, YetiRankPairwise, or PairLogitPairwise; compare against hard-binary ranking and blend only if OOF gain survives the stricter CV above.
expected_effect: Can improve local ordering among imminent stoppers without needing a full sequence model, especially in crowded pit windows.
risk: If soft labels are poorly shaped, the model may optimize broader stop-window ranking at the expense of exact next-lap discrimination.
